arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola's £33.5m gamble backfires again as Man City star 'exposed' by Real Madrid
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola named Abdukodir Khusanov, his January signing, on the right side of defence but the youngster continues to struggle as Real Madrid ran riot at the Bernabeu

arts.ttl: After hat trick, Mbappe to 'define an era' at Real
arts.sum: Kylian Mbappé said he wants to "define an era" at Real Madrid after scoring a hat trick in their 3-1 Champions League win over Manchester City on Wednesday, but insisted his season should be judged on trophies, not goals.

arts.ttl: Kylian Mbappe Becomes First Player in Champions League History to Accomplish Legendary Feat
arts.sum: Kylian Mbappé is the only player in Champions League history to score a hat trick at the Santiago Bernabéu and the Camp Nou.

arts.ttl: 'It hurt more' - Pep Guardiola reacts to Man City exit at Real Madrid
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola spoke to reporters after the team were knocked out of the Champions League

arts.ttl: Mbappé hat-trick rips apart Manchester City to send Real Madrid racing through
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola had asked his team to at least give the Santiago Bernabéu a fright; instead, they were the ones exposed to a terrifying truth, wearing a haunted hollow look. It is not just that Manchester City’s Champions League campaign is over, a Kylian Mbappé hat-trick sending Real Madrid through here; it is them. The team that Guardiola said was a machine for eight years is no more. City’s manager had said he was lying when he gave his team a 1% chance of going through, and so it proved: it was not even that high. There was no comeback, not even a glimpse, of one. No epic, just the end. When they scored in the last minute, it was greeted with ironic cheers, this stadium continuing to laugh at them as they had done for some time now. The goal did not matter: the only bad news on a perfect night was the yellow card that means Jude Bellingham will miss the first leg against Bayer Leverkusen or Atlético Madrid. Real Madrid were superb, confirming that they are candidates for the competition they consider their own. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Ancelotti: “Mbappé is the icing on Real Madrid’s cake”
arts.sum: Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti spoke after the 3-1 win over Manchester City to win 6-3 on aggregate and seal a spot in the next round of the UEFA Champions League, but particularly focusing on Kylian Mbappé as he said, “there’s the icing on the cake, which is Mbappé. If the team works, the individuals stand out.

arts.ttl: We have to learn from Champions League exit
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola says Manchester City have to accept that we were not good enough in the Champions League following our exit against Real Madrid. City are currently fourth in the Premier League and Guardiola is determined to carry on our run of qualifying for Europe’s top competition for the past 14 seasons.

arts.ttl: All Possible UEFA Champions League Round of 16 Games
arts.sum: Every possible Champions League tie for the round of 16 ahead of the draw on Feb. 21.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid star to miss 3 of the club’s next 4 games
arts.sum: La Liga giants Real Madrid are set to be without a leading member of the club’s midfield in three of their next four outings across all competitions. This comes owing…

arts.ttl: Man City miss far more than Erling Haaland vs Real Madrid to leave awkward chairman address
arts.sum: Manchester City chairman Khaldoon Al Mubarak blasted the Blues the last time they were this limp in Madrid. More tough calls are needed
